question: KCNA Advanced: Design a stateful aggregation pipeline that computes per-key running totals over a sliding 1-hour window from topic 'transactions'. The pipeline must handle consumer rebalances without duplicating or losing intermediate state, maintain sub-second recovery after any single consumer failure, and cap per-key state growth at 1 GB across the cluster. Describe your checkpointing protocol, state migration strategy during rebalance, and exactly-once emission of aggregate results to topic 'hourly-rollups'. Include the fencing mechanism for zombie consumers and a chaos engineering plan to validate correctness under partition reassignment?
answer: Use RocksDB-backed state stores per partition with incremental checkpointing to a changelog topic (min.compaction.lag.ms=5min). Employ a two-phase commit protocol via transactional producers: phase 1 pre-commits offsets and state snapshot metadata, phase 2 commits atomically after quorum-ack. During rebalance, use sticky partition assignment and warm standby replicas that consume the changelog ahead of reassignment. Fence zombies via epoch-based consumer group generation IDs incremented on each rebalance; any producer from a stale epoch is rejected by the broker. Cap state growth at 1 GB via total-count tracking and a size-aware eviction policy on the changelog's log compaction.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  A[Source: transactions topic] --> B[Partition 0 Consumer]
  A --> C[Partition 1 Consumer]
  B --> D[RocksDB State Store P0]
  C --> E[RocksDB State Store P1]
  B --> F[Transactional Producer P0]
  C --> G[Transactional Producer P1]
  D --> H[Changelog Topic P0]
  E --> I[Changelog Topic P1]
  F --> J[Sink: hourly-rollups]
  G --> J
  K[Consumer Group Coordinator] --> L{Epoch-based fence}
  L -->|Stale epoch| M[Reject producer commit]
  L -->|Current epoch| N[Allow produce]
  O[Standby Replica] --> H
  O --> I
